findings.title: US Treasury 2024 National Risk Assessments highlight professional money laundering growth
findings.summary: Treasury's 2024 assessments identify growth in professional money laundering as key vulnerability, alongside fentanyl, ransomware, and digitization risks. Affects AML pillar via professional enablers (A2). Ongoing Russia-Ukraine and Hamas conflicts shape risk environment.
findings.claim_excerpt: increased potency of ransomware attacks, the growth of professional money laundering, and continued digitization of payments

filters_applied.f2_sanctions_architecture: Russia-Ukraine war and Hamas attacks elevate sanctions evasion risks via professional networks
filters_applied.f3_enabler_jurisdiction: US correspondent banking exposes systemic vulnerabilities to pro-money laundering

findings.preliminary_assessment: Professional enablers represent under-covered architecture enabling high-volume laundering, bridging traditional finance and digital threats.
findings.confidence_basis: Primary Treasury report with detailed risk analysis

search_queries_run: US Treasury 2024 National Risk Assessments money laundering
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Official US govt report corroborated by FinCEN resources; multi-source validation.

cross_monitor_relevance.wdm: Institutional risk assessment signals state capacity limits

findings.review_reason: Assess pro-money laundering trends against Tier A enabler dynamics

findings.title: Tornado Cash developer Roman Storm trial underscores crypto sanctions evasion risks
findings.summary: Roman Storm's July 2025 trial in NY for Tornado Cash protocol alleges laundering over $1B in illicit proceeds via sanctions violations and unlicensed money transmission. Targets digital-asset threat actors (A5) in AML/CPF pillars.
findings.claim_excerpt: Storm proceeded to trial... used to anonymize more than one billion dollars in illicit proceeds

filters_applied.f2_sanctions_architecture: Crypto mixers as key node in sanctions evasion architecture
filters_applied.f3_enabler_jurisdiction: US DOJ shift to knowledge-based offenses highlights enforcement priorities

findings.preliminary_assessment: Mixing services form core dark finance infrastructure, enabling TBML and proliferation via anonymity.
findings.confidence_basis: Detailed legal analysis of ongoing DOJ case

sources_cited.excerpt: conspired to commit money laundering, violate sanctions

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Legal firm analysis + Treasury risk report; cross-validated.

findings.review_reason: Monitor DOJ crypto enforcement pivot for A5 actor trends

findings.title: FinCEN/OFAC target Huione Group as SE Asia scam conduit
findings.summary: October 2025 actions sever Huione Group (TCO) from US system, sanctioning scam laundering networks linked to SE Asia, including Prince Group overlaps. Involves Singapore-adjacent A2/A4 actors in AML.
findings.claim_excerpt: Huione as central financial conduit for Southeast Asia’s scam industry

filters_applied.f2_sanctions_architecture: Correspondent account restrictions expose SE Asia facilitation
filters_applied.f3_enabler_jurisdiction: Singapore wealth mgmt growth enables scam proceeds integration

findings.preliminary_assessment: Correspondent banking opacity sustains regional dark finance hubs.
findings.confidence_basis: Coordinated FinCEN/OFAC actions detailed in analysis

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Regulatory actions + legal recap; strong traceback.

findings.review_reason: Evaluate Singapore correspondent role in scam architecture

findings.title: Jorge Figueira indictment exposes UK/US-registered OTC/crypto laundering network
findings.summary: 2026 indictment of Venezuelan Figueira reveals pro network using UK/US OTC brokers, crypto, shells for laundering. Highlights A2 professional enablers in Tier A jurisdictions.
findings.claim_excerpt: professional laundering network using cash, crypto, OTC brokerages registered in the UK and U.S.

filters_applied.f3_enabler_jurisdiction: UK Companies House opacity enables shell integration

findings.preliminary_assessment: Professional networks bridge regulated institutions and illicit flows, under-covered TBML enabler.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Specialized org; needs court docs for Confirmed.

findings.review_reason: Prioritize A2 under-coverage; check Companies House links

findings.title: FATF July 2025 webinar updates terrorist financing risks, emphasizing online/combined methods
findings.summary: FATF's first TF report since 2015 details evolving tactics from traditional to online, combined methods. Impacts CTF pillar via A6 actors globally.
findings.claim_excerpt: Comprehensive Update on Terrorist Financing Risks... evolving tactics and use of combined methods

findings.title: West Africa oil corruption networks signal TBML risks in Tier D rotation
findings.summary: Ongoing fraud/oil bunkering in Nigeria funds ISWAP via TBML (A3/A6). Under-coverage correction highlights regulatory gaps.
